subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Composition of the Universe
description: A quick, practical guide to The Composition of the Universe.

hook.text: Everything you can see—stars, planets, people—makes up just 5% of the universe. The rest is invisible.

buildup.text: Dark energy accounts for 68%. Dark matter accounts for 27%. Ordinary matter is a 5% sliver of the cosmic pie.

discovery.text: This breakdown comes from the CMB, galaxy surveys, and supernova data. All three methods agree remarkably well.

twist.text: We've built all of science, chemistry, and biology from the 5%. The other 95% remains almost entirely mysterious.

climax.text: Understanding the 95% is the great challenge of 21st-century physics. New telescopes and detectors are leading the charge.

punchline.text: Five percent visible. Ninety-five percent unknown. Humbling math.

quiz.question: What percentage of the universe is made of ordinary (visible) matter?
